original_text: All bridge projects must contain exactly one IfcProject instance
at least one IfcSite that defines the terrain
Every bridge structure must be represented by an IfcBridge entity
Horizontal and vertical alignment curves must be defined using IfcAlignment entities
substructure of the bridge must be modeled as IfcBridgePart with predefined type SUBSTRUCTURE
superstructure of the bridge must be modeled as IfcBridgePart with predefined type SUPERSTRUCTURE
Bridge girders should be modeled as IfcElementAssembly with predefined type GIRDER
bridge deck must be modeled as IfcBridgePart with predefined type DECK
Deck segments should be modeled as IfcBridgePart with predefined type DECK_SEGMENT
Abutments at the ends of the bridge must be modeled as IfcBridgePart with predefined type ABUTMENT
Bridge piers must be modeled as IfcBridgePart with predefined type PIER
Foundation piles must be modeled as IfcPile entities
proposed ground surface must be modeled as IfcGeographicElement with predefined type TERRAIN
existing ground surface must be modeled as IfcGeographicElement with predefined type TERRAIN
Boring points indicating soil layers and properties must be modeled as IfcGeographicElement
IfcProject instance that identifies the overall project and provides defaults for units
IfcSite that defines the terrain within vicinity of the bridge project
Boring points must include the property Depth in the property set Pset_GeographicElementCommon
Bridge girders must specify material information
Foundation piles must specify material information
The site must be aggregated under the IfcProject
It must be contained within the IfcSite
It must be contained within the IfcSite
They must be contained within the IfcSite
